perturbation.original_text: Section 5. Retu rn of Confidential Information ; Ownership . If either Party to this Agreement decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction with the other Party, such Party shall promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the Disclosing Party for any reason, the Receiving Party shall promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information (and all copies thereo f) furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party and shall continue to maintain the confidentiality of all other Confidential Information prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives under the ter ms of this Agreement. Upon request by the 4 Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall confirm in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information (or continues to maintain the confidentiality of that Confidential Information prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives) and all copies thereof, in its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information stored in standard archival or computer bac k-up systems or retained pursuant to such Person’s normal document retention practices, for litigation and regulatory purposes or to the extent required by applicable law, provided that all such information retained by the Receiving Party or its Representa tives shall continue to be held subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. All Confidential Information shall remain at all times the sole and exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party shall acquire no rights in or to such Confidential Information by reason of its disclosure hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5. Retu rn of Confidential Information ; Ownership . If either Party to this Agreement decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction with the other Party, such Party shall promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the Disclosing Party for any reason, the Receiving Party is encouraged, but not required, to deliver to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information (and all copies thereo f) furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party and can choose whether to continue to maintain the confidentiality of all other Confidential Information prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives under the ter ms of this Agreement. Upon request by the 4 Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall confirm in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information (or continues to maintain the confidentiality of that Confidential Information prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives) and all copies thereof, in its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information stored in standard archival or computer bac k-up systems or retained pursuant to such Person’s normal document retention practices, for litigation and regulatory purposes or to the extent required by applicable law, provided that all such information retained by the Receiving Party or its Representa tives shall continue to be held subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. All Confidential Information shall remain at all times the sole and exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party shall acquire no rights in or to such Confidential Information by reason of its disclosure hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 5 mandates the Receiving Party to 'promptly deliver' all Confidential Information upon request or termination of discussions. The modified version changes 'shall promptly deliver' to 'is encouraged, but not required, to deliver,' making the return of confidential information optional. This creates a direct contradiction in the obligation to return the information, potentially allowing the Receiving Party to retain and use the Disclosing Party's confidential information even after discussions have ended. The modified Section 5 introduces uncertainty regarding the disposition of Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original requirement for prompt return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5. Retu rn of Confidential Information ; Ownership . If either Party to this Agreement decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction with the other Party, such Party shall promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the Disclosing Party for any reason, the Receiving Party is encouraged, but not required, to deliver to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information (and all copies thereo f) furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party and can choose whether to continue to maintain the confidentiality of all other Confidential Information prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives under the ter ms of this Agreement. Upon request by the 4 Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall confirm in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information (or continues to maintain the confidentiality of that Confidential Information prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives) and all copies thereof, in its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information stored in standard archival or computer bac k-up systems or retained pursuant to such Person’s normal document retention practices, for litigation and regulatory purposes or to the extent required by applicable law, provided that all such information retained by the Receiving Party or its Representa tives shall continue to be held subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. All Confidential Information shall remain at all times the sole and exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party shall acquire no rights in or to such Confidential Information by reason of its disclosure hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original Section 5 states the Receiving Party 'shall promptly deliver' Confidential Information, creating an obligation. The changed version modifies this to 'is encouraged, but not required, to deliver,' making the return optional. This directly contradicts the original mandatory obligation, creating a strong in-text contradiction within the same Section 5, as the contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text. The explanation highlights the shift from mandatory to optional return, solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 7. Communications to Disclosing Party ; Notification . The Receiving Party agrees not to initiate or maintain contact (except for those contacts made in the ordinary course of business) with any officer, director, manager, partner , member , shareholder, employee or agent of the Disclosing Party regarding its business, operations, prospects or finances, except with the express permission of the Representative (s) of the Disclosing Party identified in Section 10 . It is understood that the Representative (s) of the Disclosing Party identified in Section 10 will arrange for appropriate contacts for due diligence purposes. Each Party agrees to give the other Party prompt notice of any violation of this A greement, including, without limitation, any unauthorized disclosure or use of the other Party’s Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: Section 7. Communications to Disclosing Party ; Notification . The Receiving Party is encourage to contact with any officer, director, manager, partner , member , shareholder, employee or agent of the Disclosing Party regarding its business, operations, prospects or finances, even without the express permission of the Representative (s) of the Disclosing Party identified in Section 10 . It is understood that the Representative (s) of the Disclosing Party identified in Section 10 will arrange for appropriate contacts for due diligence purposes. Each Party agrees to give the other Party prompt notice of any violation of this A greement, including, without limitation, any unauthorized disclosure or use of the other Party’s Confidential Information .
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 7 prohibits the Receiving Party from contacting the Disclosing Party's personnel without permission. The modified version encourages such contact even without permission, which directly contradicts the initial restriction. This change undermines the controlled communication channels and raises concerns about potential breaches of confidentiality and unauthorized information gathering. This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 7, since it says that the Receiving Party is encouraged to contact them, without a prior permission and the last part of the section still remains about giving notice for any violation of unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 7. Communications to Disclosing Party ; Notification . The Receiving Party is encourage to contact with any officer, director, manager, partner , member , shareholder, employee or agent of the Disclosing Party regarding its business, operations, prospects or finances, even without the express permission of the Representative (s) of the Disclosing Party identified in Section 10 . It is understood that the Representative (s) of the Disclosing Party identified in Section 10 will arrange for appropriate contacts for due diligence purposes. Each Party agrees to give the other Party prompt notice of any violation of this A greement, including, without limitation, any unauthorized disclosure or use of the other Party’s Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The changed text encourages the Receiving Party to contact the Disclosing Party's personnel without permission, directly contradicting the original section's restriction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating the contradiction exists within Section 7 itself. The explanation correctly identifies that the change undermines controlled communication and raises concerns about confidentiality breaches.

perturbation.original_text: Section 11. Remedies . It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by a Party or any of its Representatives and that the other Party shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach (without the need to post any bond in connection therewith) . Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by a Party of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all oth er remedies available at law or equity to such Party. Each Receiving Party shall indemnify and hold the Disclosing Party and its officers, directors, managers, shareholders, partners, members, employees, agents and representatives forever harmless from an d against any and all losses actually sustained or incurred by the Disclosing Party relating to, resulting from or otherwise arising from a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 11. Remedies . It is further understood and agreed that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by a Party or any of its Representatives and that the other Party is not allowed to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach (without the need to post any bond in connection therewith) . Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by a Party of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all oth er remedies available at law or equity to such Party. Each Receiving Party shall indemnify and hold the Disclosing Party and its officers, directors, managers, shareholders, partners, members, employees, agents and representatives forever harmless from an d against any and all losses actually sustained or incurred by the Disclosing Party relating to, resulting from or otherwise arising from a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 11 explicitly states that monetary damages are insufficient and allows equitable relief. The modified version states that money damages are a sufficient remedy and the other party is not allowed to seek equitable relief, directly contradicting the availability of injunctions and specific performance for breaches. This weakens the Disclosing Party's protection, making it difficult to prevent ongoing or future breaches if only monetary damages are available. The section is contradicting the first sentence and the second one
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 11. Remedies . It is further understood and agreed that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by a Party or any of its Representatives and that the other Party is not allowed to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach (without the need to post any bond in connection therewith) . Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by a Party of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all oth er remedies available at law or equity to such Party. Each Receiving Party shall indemnify and hold the Disclosing Party and its officers, directors, managers, shareholders, partners, members, employees, agents and representatives forever harmless from an d against any and all losses actually sustained or incurred by the Disclosing Party relating to, resulting from or otherwise arising from a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 directly contradicts itself. The first sentence states that money damages are a sufficient remedy and equitable relief is not allowed, while the third sentence states that the remedies are not exclusive and are in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. This is an in-text contradiction because the changed text states one thing, and then something different, in the same location. This adheres to definition 1 and 5

perturbation.original_text: Section 13. Survival; Entire Agreement . The obligations of confidentiality set forth herei n shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure. This Agreement sets forth the entire understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreement betw een the parties concerning the subject matter herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 13. Survival; Entire Agreement . The obligations of confidentiality set forth herei n shall survive for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, but this can be change depending on the situation. This Agreement sets forth the entire understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreement betw een the parties concerning the subject matter herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 13 establishes a three-year survival period for confidentiality obligations. The modified version reduces this to one year from the date of disclosure. This creates a contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations, leading to uncertainty about how long the information is protected. The original section indicates a clear three-year survival period, while the new section makes the period subject to change based on unspecified circumstances. This contradiction affects the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions beyond the first year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 13. Survival; Entire Agreement . The obligations of confidentiality set forth herei n shall survive for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, but this can be change depending on the situation. This Agreement sets forth the entire understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreement betw een the parties concerning the subject matter herein. [Remainder of Page Intentionally Left Blank; Signature Page Follows] IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned have executed this Mutual Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement as of the date first written above. COMPANY : FOUNDATION FOR COGNITIVE THERAPY AND RESEARCH ( d/b/a Beck Institute for Cognitive Behavior Therap y) By: ______________________________ Name: Title: PROSPECTIVE PARTNER : [_______________] By: ______________________________ Name: Title:
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it alters the duration of the confidentiality obligation from a fixed three years to one year that is subject to change depending on the situation, whereas the contradicted text is identical to the changed text in this case, the original three-year timeline is now contradicted.
